key_points: Dorm quality varies wildly; some unrenovated buildings remain
Hot water outages reported during cold weather
Limited info on what freshmen actually get assigned
key_quotes: Don't you love it when it snows and this is just the day for the hot water to stop working in your dorm
Got Pollock my first year. It built something alright, not sure it was character though
freshman that just moved in, feeling isolated... it's hard to make friends because people don't really engage

key_points: Greek life and Wharton culture dominate social scene
Non-Wharton students report feeling like second-class citizens
Freshmen report difficulty breaking into already-formed friend groups
key_quotes: I feel like all of the friend groups have already formed from high schools and hometowns
Penn Engineering alum and I do just say 'yep, sure' when asked if I went to Wharton
Wharton students definitely have a smug look as if they know they're the Big Man on Campus

key_points: No standout praise for dining in any student posts
Saxbys closures on both ends of campus frustrated students
Food options near campus praised more than on-campus dining
key_quotes: Who's responsible for the saxbys closure... I'm so glad I'm graduating this year ughhhhh
absolute best eats at Penn? What restaurants are your favorite to go that are close to campus
why is this thing $30

key_points: Penn described as 'consistently the most depressing school in the nation'
Intense academic pressure leads to real breakdowns and self-doubt
Imposter syndrome is widespread, especially among non-Wharton students
key_quotes: As a Penn student, this is pretty accurate... consistently the most depressing school in the nation
I should have gone to my state school I can't do this
University of Pennsylvania is a mental asylum where fun goes to die and all students suffer under imposter syndrome

key_points: Quaker Commitment raised threshold to $200K for full tuition—genuinely helpful
Families below $200K still receiving aid packages far below expectations
Financial aid appeals frequently rejected with no clear recourse
key_quotes: Penn advertises that families making around 200k receive full tuition, yet we are well below that and still did not get an offer close to full tuition
Is it normal to pay 99k annually to go to UPenn as a premed with family income being one parent making 200k? My financial aid appeal got rejected
Penn will no longer consider the value of the primary family home among assets

key_points: Wharton is genuinely world-class; other schools play second fiddle
Non-Wharton programs are good but not exceptional by Ivy standards
Grade shock is real—Wharton freshmen report being blindsided by midterms
key_quotes: If you're not a Wharton student, then you will always play second fiddle to Wharton students—in the eyes of the university, potential employers, and your peers
I'm a freshman at Wharton who thought getting 3.8+ GPA was a piece of cake until midterms cooked me
When I was at Penn, there essentially was no music department, just the marching band and a bunch of acapella groups

key_points: Data breaches hit GSE email system; donor records also reportedly stolen
Title IX resolution with federal government signals past compliance issues
Penn rejected White House compact on academic freedom—praised by faculty
key_quotes: Penn hacker claims to have stolen 1.2 million donor records in data breach
Penn's Title IX Resolution with the U.S. Department of Education Office for Civil Rights
Penn rejects White House proposal for special funding treatment

key_points: West Philly location means real safety concerns just blocks from campus
Unprovoked attacks and harassment reported near 37th and Walnut
Campus itself is beautiful; University City neighborhood is the actual issue
key_quotes: Officials warn of unprovoked attacks near Penn's campus in Philadelphia's University City section
7 yrs apart and was welcomed by rocks thrown at me... 37th and Walnut
What's going on with safety around Penn lately? My scooter got stolen on Chestnut, and a couple of days later I was shoved by a group of juveniles

key_points: Wharton grads get elite IB/consulting placements; others see less benefit
Penn Engineering and CAS alumni network is solid but less dominant
Internship culture is intense and somewhat secretive even among students
key_quotes: How do Penn students actually find all these internships and research gigs?? Everything's so hush hush
It feels like everyone's super lowkey about how they got them
Private Equity and Hedge funds placing in NYC or London easily pay $400-500k all in

key_points: Full-pay families near $200K face $99K+/year with no aid relief
Strong ROI for Wharton specifically; weaker case for other schools
Multiple students chose Penn over free rides elsewhere and regretted it
key_quotes: Is it normal to pay 99k annually to go to UPenn as a premed with family income being one parent making 200k
UPenn full pay vs UNC Full Ride... I actually committed to UNC
I chose elitness/prestige over what my heart was telling me to do, pick Michigan. I have no clue still if I made the correct decision

key_points: Prestige-obsessed culture, especially around Wharton, alienates many students
Students who embrace Penn's identity love it; those who don't, struggle
Politically turbulent right now with federal funding threats and campus controversies
key_quotes: Penn is not in the middle of the hood like USC. It's right next to center city and west Philly which isn't quite dangerous until you go about 20 more blocks
Mostly because of Wharton, we might be the biggest a holes in the Ivy league (and that rubs off on a lot of us)
we are also somehow always in the headlines of many controversial news and is consistently the most depressing school in the nation

key_points: Safety: unprovoked street attacks reported within blocks of main campus
Mental Health: students openly say Penn is 'the most depressing school in the nation'
Data Security: hacked GSE emails, 1.2M donor records stolen, identity theft reports from students
key_quotes: Someone gained access to send email through Penn's list serve... there goes my SSN
Penn hacker claims to have stolen 1.2 million donor records in data breach
Anyone experiencing identity theft today? Someone hacked into my Grubhub and ordered multiple meals... I received 75 emails this evening with authentication codes

key_points: Philadelphia Museum of Art offers $25 student memberships—underused stress relief
Penn-built course planning tools like PennCoursePlan are genuinely excellent
City access gives unmatched internship proximity to NYC, DC, and Philly firms
key_quotes: If Penn is making you anxious, I highly recommend getting yourself an art museum membership
UPenNow pulls from multiple Penn sources and shows events happening on campus today on a map
Penn's location in Philly gives connections with 3 major cities—good for internships
